title: COVID RUSH
desc: An indie game mechanics exploration built in Unity, turning a chaotic supply run into a fast, satirical 3D dash. Players navigate between safe (green) and epidemic (red) zones that shift with the BGM, collecting masks and vaccines while avoiding AI-controlled infected avatars.

content.heading: Introduction
content.body:
【Immerse Experience】
The story fits current reality. Plots, music, and game mode will give players nervous feelings and excitement. They can easily immerse themselves in the game world, and have similar but more exciting experiences different from reality.

【New Game Mode】
Players can not only interact with NPCs but also collect supplies in game. There are several significant characteristics in our game, such as music switches terrains combined with Tag's elements, and getting to the safe regions to avoid infected, etc.

【Fascinating Scenes】
Because changing terrains and safe regions is the main element in COVID RUSH. There will be several fascinating scenes switching during the game.

content.heading: Game Story
content.body:
Severe disease has stroken the world for a period of time. The governments of countries try hard to have citizens vaccinated. They also strongly appeal to their citizens to wear masks in order to protect themselves and others.

You have gradually noticed the situation of spreading disease, and you also find out that some regions are severe and some are not. People tend to get rid of dangerous regions and swarm into safe regions. While they enter the safe region, they still try to grab more supplies they can.

Watching those raving behavior, you are conscious of making yourself out of threat right now!

content.heading: How to Play
content.body: On the ground in the game scene, there will be squares colored in red or green. Red ones represent epidemic areas, and green ones are safe areas. Squares will change color with the BGM. Players will use a keyboard (WASD or arrow keys) to control the characters to move. If the player is in green areas, he will need to collect items (masks and vaccines). If the player is in red areas, he will need to leave as soon as possible, and if he doesn't do so, the items will be eliminated, and lead to the game's ending (game over). Note that NPCs may interfere with you and take your items away.

content.heading: Game System
content.body:
1. Player avatar: Players can control the avatar by a keyboard to interact with the game objects.
2. Squares: Different colors of squares have different effects to players, and it will change colors along with music.
3. Item: Items will be randomly generated in green squares, and players need to interact with them.
4. Game AI: The avatars controlled by game AI can infect players, and make the game more intense and difficult.

categoryLabel: VR x Hand Tracking x Escape Room
title: 深海低吟 | Deep In Sea
desc: A VR escape room experience built in Unity with Oculus Quest 2 hand tracking. Players take on the role of an archaeologist who awakens inside a mysterious Victorian-era ship, solving four puzzles involving hand gesture recognition, magical drawing, lightsaber combat, and branching story paths to escape a mermaid's curse.

content.heading: Story Outline
content.body:
After diving into the depths of the sea, the player—an archaeologist—hears a hauntingly beautiful song before gradually losing consciousness. Upon waking, they find themselves inside an unfamiliar ship cabin.

Solving four intricate puzzles triggers the appearance of a figure claiming to be the captain of this ancient vessel. The captain speaks of the mermaids' wicked nature and grants the player a powerful artifact to counter them. In an instant, the once-bright room turns dark and eerie.

Just then, a statue in the corner comes to life, introducing itself as a prince of the Russian Empire. He pleads with the player to help find his beloved, who was also trapped by the mermaids.

Following the clues, the player enters a mystical mirror, where they witness the prince and princess's cherished memories. As the truth unfolds, the princess bestows a key to escape the ship's cabin. But as the player steps out from the mirror's world, a crazed mermaid suddenly appears.

Story Branches:
Path A: Draw a magic circle to defeat the mermaid.
Path B: Use a hidden incantation found in the puzzles to immobilize the mermaid.

content.heading: Development Tools
content.body:
I. Game Engine: Unity
II. Models and Design: Blender, Vroid, Photoshop & Illustrator
III. Device: Oculus Quest 2

content.heading: Techniques
content.body:
I. Unity — Keyword Recognize: UnityEngine.Windows.Speech
II. Hand tracking & Hand gesture recognition:
  • Item Pickup — Using Unity's physics system for realistic physical collisions
  • Mirror Gestures — Detecting hand skeleton positions to identify gestures
  • Magical Drawing with a Pen — Forming a pen-holding position by touching index finger and thumb, integrated with Unity's line renderer system
  • Lightsaber & Backpack Access — Two buttons, one on each hand, for summoning lightsaber and opening backpack panel

content.heading: Visual Effects
content.body:
• Particles System
• PBR Shader
• VFX Shader

content.heading: Design
content.body:
• Vroid: Character model design and creation
• Photoshop & Illustrator: Game logo design, UI art used in the game

title: Time Lapse
desc: A sound art and audio-visual piece combining media transcription, modulation, and synesthetic experimentation. Using Ableton effects for sound modulation (echo, layering, repetition) and TouchDesigner for real-time audiovisual generation, the work reflects on the emotional arc of the pandemic—from anxiety to adaptation and coexistence.

content.heading: Introduction
content.body: As the pandemic surged, chaos over vaccines and waves of rumors spread. Two years passed in the blink of an eye. People moved from initial anxiety and distress to a state of calm, adapting to life under self-quarantine at home, standing in solidarity with frontline medical workers and emergency responders to combat the crisis together.

content.heading: Techniques
content.body: For the sound materials, I incorporated news reports and various foley sounds from Freesound. Using Ableton's effects, I applied modulation techniques such as echo, layering, and repetition to enhance the emotional depth. For the synesthetic experience, I utilized TouchDesigner to generate audiovisual elements, providing viewers with a dynamic visual perception.

content.heading: Sections
content.body:
1. In the darkness, ethereal and elusive lines shift unpredictably, gradually transforming in shape—like a virus lying in wait, silently lurking among the crowd.
2. Separation, infiltration, and fusion—much like human society, viruses exhibit similar behaviors, even transcending species boundaries.
3. A slow descent into struggle—a suffocating tug-of-war, not only against an invisible enemy but also against humanity's own interests and moral values.
4. The confusion surrounding virus mutations and the shortage of vaccines fueled a whirlwind of public discourse.
5. Even as cracks appear in human society, we continue to coexist.

exhibition.name: NYCU x TNUA【瘋城 This Crazy City】Online Headphone Concert
exhibition.institution: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University (NYCU) & National Taiwan University of Arts (TNUA)

title: 墨染熹微 | Smudge of Dawn
desc: A GLSL generative art piece inspired by karesansui (dry landscape gardens) and traditional Chinese ink painting. The 'ground'—represented by white sand and water patterns—is generated through trigonometric functions applied to screen coordinates. Time-based variations create ripples that extend and flow like organic ink brush strokes, with granular sand textures and three white stones guiding the composition.

content.heading: Introduction
content.body:
枯山水，以山石白砂為主體，仿照自然的山海大川，從而實現「無水而喻水」的效果。本作品以GLSL為創作工具，並以傳統中國水墨畫為質地，繪製出墨色暈染山中微弱晨光的景色。

Karesansui, or dry landscape gardens, use rocks and white sand to emulate vast mountains and rivers, achieving the effect of "expressing water without water." This work is created using GLSL, incorporating the texture of traditional Chinese ink painting to depict a scene where soft morning light gently diffuses over the mountains.

content.heading: Technique
content.body:
畫面中，白砂水紋的「地」，是以三角函數對於畫面的座標運算出「波」的形體，並加入時間的變化，使水紋蔓延、甚至延伸至「天」，以增添畫面「生」和水墨繪製的感受，而後對水紋表面加入擾動使之有砂石的顆粒感，並在場地中融入三顆白石作為山石的導流。

In the composition, the "ground"—represented by the white sand and water patterns—is generated through trigonometric functions applied to the screen coordinates, forming wave-like shapes. By introducing time-based variations, the ripples extend and flow, even merging into the "sky," enhancing the sense of movement and evoking the organic feel of ink painting. Disturbances are added to the ripple surfaces, imbuing them with a granular, sand-like texture. Additionally, three white stones are placed within the scene, guiding the flow and serving as mountain elements.

title: UE4 Workshop: 恬 / 霽月 / 歸
desc: A trilogy of digital landscapes created during a one-month UE4 workshop, building mountains, snow, water, sky, physics, wind fields, post-processing, and rendering from scratch. '恬' is inspired by Zhuge Liang's philosophy of tranquility; '霽月' projects emotions onto environments; '歸' explores homecoming through sunset hues.

content.heading: Workshop Overview
content.body:
一個月的UE4工作坊，從無到有建立山體、雪景、水體、天空、物理、風場、後期、渲染輸出。

One-month UE4 Workshop — from scratch to completion: building mountains, snowy landscapes, water bodies, sky, physics, wind fields, post-processing, and rendering output.

content.heading: ⟪恬⟫
content.body:
非淡泊無以明志，非寧靜無以致遠
－摘自 諸葛孔明《誡子書》

"Without tranquility, one cannot clarify one's purpose; without serenity, one cannot reach far."
— from Zhuge Liang, Letter of Admonition to His Son

content.heading: ⟪霽月⟫
content.body:
寓情於景
借景喻情

Projecting emotions onto the environment, borrowing landscapes to convey feeling.

content.heading: ⟪歸⟫
content.body:
斜陽於我，是「回家」

The setting sun, to me, means "coming home."

title: GLSL Shaders Collection
desc: A collective series of graphics programming pieces exploring natural phenomena: 1. Waves (Voronoi/Metaballs/Ukiyo-e style), 2. Uncertain, 3. Breathing Light, 4. Reflection.

content.heading: Collection
content.body:
1. Waves — #waves #voronoi #metaballs #浮世絵

2. Uncertain

3. Breathing Light

4. Reflection

title: 匯聚的思念 | The Convergence of Longing
desc: Using Vermeer's painting 'Girl with a Pearl Earring' as the foundation, this work explores Brownian Motion and Random Walk algorithms to irregularly visualize the convergence of longing, remembrance, and digital dispersion.

content.heading: Introduction
content.body:
以維梅爾的畫作「戴珍珠耳環的少女」(Girl with a Pearl Earring)為底，嘗試以不規則的方式呈現匯聚的思念。

Using Vermeer's painting Girl with a Pearl Earring as the foundation, this work explores an irregular approach to visualizing the convergence of longing and remembrance.

title: 隧歲 | The Tunnel
desc: A point cloud animation centered around Hsinchu's Zhongzheng Road Night Market, scanned into a point cloud model. Atmospheric animations are created separately for daytime and nighttime, incorporating mist, lighting variations, color shifts, particle dynamics, post-processing, and field-recorded sounds to convey an audiovisual experience that traverses time and space in Hsinchu's old town.

content.heading: Introduction
content.body:
「年年歲歲花相似，歲歲年年人不同。」－摘自 劉希夷〈白頭吟〉

隧，寬廣卻非無限的空間。歲，聚焦在一日的時間流動。

歷史遺留在新竹的某幾個角落，舊城區依傍在新竹市區周邊，新竹第一信用合作社、影像博物館、新竹州廳、新竹美術館...
走過繁榮與衰落，看著人群來回又離去，舊城區依舊在原地送走舊人、迎接新人，彷彿培育著、呼喚著這塊土地、這塊土地的人們。

content.heading: Technique
content.body:
本作品以中正台夜市為主場景，將其掃描成點雲模型，分別以白天與夜晚的氛圍創作動畫特效，例如霧氣、光線、顏色變化、粒子的動態與樣式、影像後期處理等，並加入實境採樣的聲響，透過白天與夜晚的氛圍差異，賦予場域穿越時間與空間的視覺觀賞與聽覺感受。

This work centers around Zhongzheng Road Night Market, scanned into a point cloud model. Atmospheric animations and effects are created separately for daytime and nighttime, incorporating mist, lighting variations, color shifts, particle dynamics, and post-processing. Field-recorded sounds are integrated to emphasize the contrast between day and night, offering an audiovisual experience that traverses both time and space.

title: はる | Haru
desc: An atmospheric exploration created in Unreal Engine 5 investigating themes of fading memory. 'Who is calling? While Haru has withered away... through the pulse of sakura.' Powered by Niagara particle systems and custom material shaders.

content.heading: Introduction
content.body:
who is calling?
while Haru has withered away.

through the pulse of sakura.

title: 植•栽 | Planted for Planting
desc: An immersive puzzle-solving game built in Unreal Engine 5. Players explore a maze, solve planting puzzles (hint: related to number of leaves), and nurture the World Tree. Combines maze exploration with open environments, utilizing Niagara particle systems to symbolize environmental restoration and the continuation of life.

content.heading: Core Gameplay
content.body:
• Maze Exploration
Players start in a complex maze and must find an exit. The maze may include mechanisms, hidden passages, or interactive environmental elements, testing the player's observation.

• Puzzle-Solving & Planting
After escaping the maze, players enter an open puzzle-solving area where they must locate suitable planting spots. (Hint: Related to the number of leaves)

• World Tree Growth
Once all suitable planting areas are correctly cultivated, the player's efforts will lead to the birth of the World Tree. The tree gradually grows, symbolizing the player's exploration and puzzle-solving journey.

content.heading: Design Goals
content.body:
• Level Design — Combining maze structures with open areas to enhance spatial planning and visual storytelling.

• Interaction Design — Creating engaging environmental interactions that enhance the puzzle-solving experience.

• Procedural Art (Using Niagara System) — Utilizing procedural techniques to enhance visual effects, particularly in plant growth.

The ultimate goal is to solve all puzzles and successfully nurture the World Tree, symbolizing environmental restoration and the continuation of life.

content.heading: Art Style & Atmosphere
content.body:
• The maze environment features a monochromatic aesthetic, using simple lighting to create an enclosed but not oppressive space.
• The puzzle-solving area is more open, fostering a sense of interaction with the environment.
• During the World Tree's growth, Niagara System particle effects enhance the visual impact.

title: 緒絮 | Threads of Thought
desc: This work centers around the visualization of brain consciousness space, connecting Arduino with UE5 to create visuals that model rational chemical molecular movements (biological neural transmission) and emotional, abstract imaginative spaces. Simple sensors serve as interactive devices to realize human stimuli in this brain consciousness space as a medium.

content.heading: Introduction
content.body:
With the rise of the metaverse, Virtual Reality (VR), Augmented Reality (AR), and Mixed Reality (MR) have emerged rapidly, applied in navigation, entertainment, virtual events, video content, educational training, language translation, sensing, tracking, and more. These technologies have even advanced into the integration of VR, AR, and MR into Extended Reality (XR).

As we actively strive to "break" the two-dimensional space of computer screens and attempt to integrate the two-dimensional virtual world into our three-dimensional reality, one may ask: Is it not the three-dimensional reality that is being absorbed into the two-dimensional world of computers, with human consciousness and emotional experiences gradually being influenced by this virtual two-dimensional world?

This work centers around the visualization of brain consciousness space, connecting Arduino with UE5 to create a visual effect that models rational chemical molecular movements (biological neural transmission) and emotional, abstract imaginative spaces. It also attempts to use simple sensors as interactive devices to realize human stimuli in this brain consciousness space as a medium.

title: 存在觀察 | Existence Observation
desc: A two-device, four-stage multimedia installation. Device one uses a 'window' (door) as a symbolic visual medium, with the exhibition space recreated as a point cloud model in Unreal Engine 5. When viewers pause before it, they glimpse the invisible particles—data, consciousness, imagination—that construct human experience. Device two presents 'whispers' on the ground. Stages progress from an inviting tunnel of light, to a theatrical dawn, to energy and existence, and finally to consciousness and emotion.

content.heading: Concept
content.body:
現實世界的物質常以粒子的形式構成，這些粒子如同光束、塵埃一般存於空間，可能緊密、可能疏離，感覺不存在於何方、卻又無所不在，這些粒子是數據、意識、想像和祈望等不可見事物，以緩慢且難以察覺的方式存在且變化著，共同建構人類的真實經驗。

The material of the real world is often composed of particles, which, like beams of light or dust, exist in space—dense or distant, seemingly nowhere yet omnipresent. They represent data, consciousness, imagination, and wishes, existing and changing in slow, imperceptible ways, together constructing the human experience of reality.

content.heading: Installation Structure
content.body:
本作品分為兩個裝置構成、四個階段的影像。
裝置一以「窗」（門）作為一種象徵性的視覺媒介，並以展覽場地「藝空間」為點雲模型基礎，利用虛幻引擎虛構此空間，若觀者駐足停留，才能透過「窗」一窺到這些原先無法單憑肉眼察覺的存在，並與之共鳴。
裝置二為地面的「絮語」，來自這扇「窗」（門）透露出這個空間所存在的意念。

The work consists of two devices and four stages of imagery. Device one uses the "window" (door) as a symbolic visual medium, with the exhibition space recreated as a point cloud model in Unreal Engine. When the observer pauses and stays, they glimpse the invisible existences through the "window" and resonate with them. Device two is the "whisper" on the ground, revealing the ideas existing in this space.

content.heading: Four Stages
content.body:
Stage Zero: When no one is present, it is a passage—light circles from near to far, drawing people in.

Stage One: Door (Theater Opening)
theater, dawn, optical light, tunnel, spotlight, fog, mist, outdoor, abstract

Stage Two: Energy (Sense of Existence)
energy, existence, voice, sound, religion, soul, fluid, abstract, cosmos, galaxy, nerve, line, smoke

Stage Three: Consciousness, Emotion (Composition)
language, thought, consciousness, emotion, floating, mind, line, mist, flow, desaturate, abstract

exhibition.name: 虛構｜The Pledge｜L'Engagement 物質性演算美學展
exhibition.location: NYCU Yang Ming Campus, College of Humanities and Social Sciences, Zhi Xing Art Space
exhibition.event: Opening and Immaculate Dance Theater Forum: 10/6 12:00-15:30

title: 痕跡 | Traces
desc: Drawing inspiration from ceramic crazing (開片) technique, this work explores the aesthetic dialogue between temporality and human intervention. Cracks are treated as tangible representations of time, simulated via Quadtree algorithms and procedural generation. The work extends crack expressions into diverse visual forms including crystal engraving, ripples, and skin meridians, liberating cracks from the traditional ceramic framework into contemporary digital art. Keywords: Digital Art, Quadtree, Crazing, Multimedia.

content.heading: Concept
content.body:
《痕跡》從陶瓷開片工藝中汲取靈感，探索時間性與人為之間的美學對話。開片裂紋自然生成於陶瓷表面，其紋理如同時間的筆觸，描繪著自然與器物間的深層互動，承載豐厚的文化與歷史意涵。透過數位化形式，作品重新詮釋開片的時間性與審美特質，聚焦時間作用與人為介入所留下的痕跡。

"Traces" draws inspiration from the ceramic crazing technique to investigate the aesthetic interplay between temporality and human intervention. Crazing, with its naturally formed cracks on the surface of ceramics, serves as a visual manifestation of time, portraying the deep interaction between nature and objects, while carrying rich cultural and historical significance.

content.heading: Crack as Time
content.body:
《痕跡》將裂紋視為時間的具象化，模擬自然紋理隨時間累積的過程，並融入人為操作所賦予的獨特性，每一道裂紋如同風化的軌跡，記錄過往的記憶與變遷。作品以裂紋生成的動態變化為核心，展現隨時序推進的視覺演化，深刻詮釋開片工藝的邏輯與美學。

This work views cracks as a tangible representation of time, simulating the accumulation of natural patterns over time while incorporating the uniqueness introduced by human intervention. Each crack serves as a weathered trace, recording past memories and transformations.

content.heading: Extended Expressions
content.body:
作品進一步延伸裂紋的表現，創造多樣的視覺形式，包括水晶內雕、水波紋與皮膚經絡等，將裂紋從傳統陶瓷開片的框架中解放，置於多元載體之上，結合數位媒材與程序化生成的特性，拓展裂紋作為時間與文化象徵的表現可能性。

The work extends the expression of cracks by creating diverse visual forms, such as crystal engraving, ripples, and skin meridians. These cracks are liberated from the traditional ceramic framework and placed on various media, combining digital media and procedural generation to expand the expressive possibilities of cracks as symbols of time and culture.

關鍵字：數位藝術、四元樹、陶瓷開片、多媒體
Keywords: Digital Art, Quadtree, Crazing, Aesthetic, Multimedia

exhibition.name: GDAP#13 閾質 (LIMINAL)
exhibition.location: No. 256, Section 1, Dihua Street, Datong District, Taipei City (Near Daqiaotou MRT Station)

note: Paper submissions have not been made public yet.

title: 水族魚生 | AquaVerse
desc: An augmented reality mobile application where viewers use smartphones to explore an underwater world. Users interact with fish on the screen, and an introduction panel for each fish dynamically appears on the mobile device, providing knowledge about different species in an engaging and immersive way.

content.heading: Concept
content.body:
• Educational Significance
Given the observation that augmented reality can be applied to educational development, daily life assistance, and more, how to integrate technology into people's lives in a way that enhances experiences and benefits society has always been a subject of discussion and practice.

• Technological Interaction
The dissemination of knowledge primarily relies on accessible content materials. With advancements in technologies like 5G networks and the metaverse, dynamic content such as videos and animations tend to capture viewers' attention and facilitate learning more effectively.

content.heading: Introduction
content.body: By developing augmented reality software, viewers can use their smartphones without the need for additional equipment to explore an underwater world. Moreover, they can interact with the fish on the screen, gaining knowledge about different species in an engaging and immersive way.

content.heading: Prototype
content.body:
• Click on the main page and start the game
• Select a fish in the environment (AR), and the introduction panel for the fish will appear on the mobile device

categoryLabel: AR Mixed Image Recognition & AIoT Game
title: 垃圾車等等我 | Garbage Truck, Wait for Me
desc: A home fitness game app developed through industry-school collaboration with Field Technology Ltd. and NOBOLLEL. Using AR mixed image recognition and AIoT rapid game module construction, the app uses motion sensing via smartphone cameras to detect body movements combined with gamification. Inspired by the Taiwanese cultural experience of chasing the garbage truck, the game makes fitness accessible, fun, and equipment-free for all ages.

content.heading: Introduction
content.body:
由田野科技(Field Technology Ltd.)專案開發小組帶領2位研習生運用公司已建置AR混合影像辨識、AIOT快速建構遊戲模組，透過與NOBOLLEL合作共同開發自製年度遊戲雛型。目前已完成企劃2款居家健身遊戲App——【垃圾車等等我】與【打擊垃圾車】，專案開發小組已完成開發，並於2022年11月30日上架OB版。

The project development team (Field Technology Ltd.) led two interns in utilizing the company's established AR mixed image recognition and AIoT rapid game module construction, co-developing the annual game prototype in collaboration with NOBOLLEL. Two home fitness game apps have been developed: [Garbage Truck, Wait for Me] and [Smash the Garbage Truck], launched as OB version on November 30, 2022.

content.heading: Game Planning Conceptual Framework
content.body:
• Fitness and exercise have become a trend in recent years, advocated for all ages. During the pandemic, maintaining health is important, but many can't go outdoors or to the gym.

• People want to maintain an exercise habit but don't want to spend a lot of money on large fitness equipment, and may not have enough space at home.

• After conducting market research and discussing with the development engineering team, we used image recognition technology to detect body movements, combined with gamification, to create a home fitness game app that is easily accessible.

• Initially we considered incorporating AIoT or fitness accessories, but considering the added cost and complexity, we focused on image recognition and motion sensing as the core foundation.

• The game needs to be fun, involve full-body movement, and be suitable for all ages. For local Taiwanese culture, a game concept that would be quickly understood is chasing the garbage truck!

content.heading: Download & Usage
content.body: It is recommended that the mobile phone can project to a projector or TV for a larger screen, using the phone's rear camera for gameplay. PCs or laptops can also project to a larger screen, with the built-in or external webcam used for gameplay.

title: 洄鄉 | Echo-me
desc: An interactive installation interweaving silhouettes of humans and fish into a species fusion—'human but not human, fish but not fish.' Abstract contours depict a 'homeward migration' across urban landscapes and shimmering waters. The installation detects viewers' presence and generates new on-screen characters that join the procession, symbolizing the integration and continuity of human history with nature.

content.heading: Introduction
content.body:
《洄鄉》以人與魚等物種的身影交織，牽動彼此，表現一種「是人非人，是魚亦非魚」的物種融合，彷彿一種無形的力量在所有生命中悄然流動，交錯成共生的網絡，意欲傳達洄游與回鄉的過程不僅是動植物的本能，也包含人類對源頭的回歸與尋找。

Echo-me interweaves the silhouettes of humans and fish, merging them into a fusion of species that is "human but not human, fish but not fish." It conveys an invisible yet powerful force that flows quietly through all of life, intricately intertwining them into a network of co-existence.

content.heading: Interaction
content.body:
本作品以抽象輪廓勾勒物種型態，並於都市之景及水光之隙緩慢行走的行列表現物種「洄鄉」，影像如水波般延展，傳遞回溯源頭的力量。當觀眾站立於作品前，他們將成為這場討論的一部分，透過裝置偵測觀眾、於畫面中生成新角色，而後與物種行列一同前行，象徵人類歷史與自然的共融與延續。

Using abstract contours to outline the forms of species, the work depicts their "homeward migration" as they slowly traverse the urban landscape and shimmering waters. When viewers stand before the work, they become part of this discussion—the installation detects their presence and generates new on-screen characters that join the procession, symbolizing the integration and continuity of human history with nature.

exhibition.name: 2025六燃文件展：洄游六燃。洄游頭前溪
exhibition.location: B1, Haoran Library, NYCU Hsinchu Campus

title: Digital Crazing: Exploring the Application of Quadtree Structure in Time and User Intervention
desc: A novel tool for generating crazing patterns that explores the relationship between time and user intervention. Accepted to the SIGGRAPH 2025 Art Papers Program and published in the PACM-CGIT Journal. The tool employs hierarchical depth slicing to simulate crazing evolution, combining natural crack progression with human-shaped patterns through interactive shadow-based dye simulation.

content.heading: Abstract
content.body:
We present a novel tool for generating crazing patterns that explores the relationship between time and user intervention. Inspired by the formation of crazing during firing, this tool reinterprets this phenomenon, which has evolved into a culturally significant aesthetic.

The tool employs a hierarchical depth slicing technique to simulate the evolution of crazing, showcasing the transition from prominent fissures to intricate patterns. It combines the natural progression of cracks over time with those shaped by human intervention, capturing the underlying logic of crazing formation.

content.heading: Interactive Mechanism
content.body:
Our interactive work exploits the user intervention characteristics, where the crazing changes in response to the duration of the observer's shadow. This simulation highlights how coarse cracks are more prone to absorb dye, while fine cracks resist coloration.

The observer's shadow is conceptualized as the "dye," emphasizing the contrast between human and natural influences over time. This design amplifies the relationship between temporality and human intervention, offering viewers a novel aesthetic experience.

content.heading: Contribution & Future Work
content.body:
This work integrates cultural context and artistic theory, digitizes the logic behind crazing formation, and explores the aesthetic significance of material transformation. The tool framework dynamically presents the interaction between time and human influence by simulating dye deposition, temporal progression, and crazing formation.

In future work, we aim to enhance the tool performance by developing plugins for relevant design software, expanding applications in interactive design and digital creation, opening new possibilities for digital material generation techniques.

title: 雨霾 II | Rain Haze II
desc: Co-developed the performance interface for Rain Haze II at LuxuryLogico's solo exhibition 'Cosmic Sketches.' The core concept maps image grayscale values directly to motor speeds, turning rigid hardware control into intuitive visual-to-tactile manipulation. Adjusting grayscale and opacity of control points simulates storm clouds, heavy rain, and deep thunder—pixels on screen instantly translate into the rhythmic striking of marine debris.

content.heading: Project Overview
content.body:
Rain Haze II is part of LuxuryLogico's solo exhibition「宇宙寫生 Cosmic Sketches」, running from 2025/10/04 to 2026/01/11.

I co-developed the performance interface with motion designer Yi CHEN. The core of this project was turning rigid hardware control into something intuitive: mapping image grayscale values directly to motor speeds.

content.heading: Technical Approach
content.body:
While the technical logic is straightforward, the visual-to-tactile control is remarkably direct. By adjusting the grayscale and opacity of control points, I can simulate the sensation of storm clouds, heavy rain, and even the deep rumble of thunder.

Watching the pixels on the screen instantly translate into the rhythmic striking of marine debris was a unique experience. This "image-as-power" synchronization felt incredibly fresh—definitely a standout highlight of the whole production.

exhibition.name: 宇宙寫生 Cosmic Sketches — 豪華朗機工個展 The LuxuryLogico Exhibition

title: 天氣好不好我們都要飛 | Fly! No Matter What
desc: Designed and built the website for LuxuryLogico's 'Fly! No Matter What' project—a 15-year dream reaching its latest chapter in the 2025 'Cosmic Sketches' exhibition. The interface captures the shift from 'power of the flock' to 'individual skies,' letting every bird fly solo in its own space while also soaring together. Built from scratch in one month: database, filters, artwork archives, backend management, and email functions.

content.heading: Project Overview
content.body: The story of "Fly! No Matter What" started 15 years ago, and the 2025 "Cosmic Sketches" is the latest chapter of that dream. Working on the website design and construction, my goal was to build a digital space that could hold all the vibrant dreams and memories of every newly drawn bird.

content.heading: Interface Design
content.body: For the interface, I wanted to capture the shift from the "power of the flock" to "individual skies." I focused on making sure every bird could fly solo in its own pure space, but also join the others to soar together across different skies.

content.heading: Development
content.body: This was my first time diving into a full-system build from scratch—handling the database, filters, and email functions—all within a one-month deadline. Even though there's still room for improvement, building this from the ground up, from the artwork archives to the backend management, was a challenging and rewarding experience.

exhibition.name: 宇宙寫生 Cosmic Sketches — 豪華朗機工個展 The LuxuryLogico Exhibition

title: 手識 | Hand Knowledge
desc: Developed the gesture recognition system for '手識,' where gestures serve as a bridge between consciousness and the digital environment. Using TouchDesigner MediaPipe for real-time hand skeleton tracking, skeletal angles are calculated and streamed via UDP to drive Blender model deformations, then handed off to 3D animators for final refinement.

content.heading: Concept
content.body: In the work《手識》, gestures are explored as a bridge between consciousness and the digital environment. I was responsible for developing the gesture recognition system.

content.heading: Technical Workflow
content.body: I used TouchDesigner MediaPipe to achieve real-time hand skeleton tracking. After aligning the bone with a Blender model, calculated skeletal angles are streamed via UDP protocol to drive the model's deformation, then handed off to 3D animators for final refinement.

exhibition.name: 宇宙寫生 Cosmic Sketches — 豪華朗機工個展 The LuxuryLogico Exhibition

title: 最後一問 | THE LAST QUESTION
desc: R&D for LuxuryLogico's unmanned theater production 'THE LAST QUESTION' at 2026 NTT Arts NOVA. Responsible for designing the gesture dynamics recognition system, focusing on dual-hand motion capture with real-time skeletal angle streaming into Blender. Although joint occlusion from single-webcam input prevented final-show deployment, the stress testing of dual-hand recognition on edge devices remains a solid technical milestone.

content.heading: Project Background
content.body: During the R&D process for LuxuryLogico's unmanned theater production, THE LAST QUESTION, presented at 2026 NTT Arts NOVA (2026/04/15 – 2026/04/19), I was primarily responsible for the design of the gesture dynamics recognition system.

content.heading: Technical Approach
content.body: Building upon previous hand-tracking research, the technical focus this time was on dual-hand motion capture, with the original plan being to recognize skeletal angles and stream them into Blender in real-time for dynamic application.

content.heading: Findings & Outcome
content.body:
During the preliminary research phase, the use of a single webcam as the primary sensor meant that joint occlusion led to skeletal data that deviated too significantly from actual movements, failing to provide the intended efficiency for the production workflow. After evaluation, it was decided to keep this functionality in the pre-development stage rather than implementing it in the final show.

Although it didn't make it to the live environment, the stress testing of dual-hand recognition on edge devices remains a solid technical milestone.

exhibition.name: 2026 NTT Arts NOVA — 豪華朗機工《最後一問》THE LAST QUESTION

title: 最後一問：光球模擬 | THE LAST QUESTION: Light Sphere Simulation
desc: Developed the 3D dynamic simulation system of the light sphere for LuxuryLogico's 'THE LAST QUESTION' at 2026 NTT Arts NOVA. Built on Unreal Engine with custom light function shaders to simulate multi-directional spotlights, real-time RGB extraction from external video mapped to physical LED nodes, and multi-source motion asset integration for synchronized lighting and mechanical displacement pre-visualization.

content.heading: Project Overview
content.body: In the development of THE LAST QUESTION for LuxuryLogico, presented at 2026 NTT Arts NOVA (2026/04/15 – 2026/04/19), I was responsible for the 3D dynamic simulation system of the light sphere, focusing on the integration of dynamic simulation and data output.

content.heading: Technical Pipeline
content.body:
The development pipeline was built on Unreal Engine, utilizing custom light function shaders to simulate multi-directional spotlights with projection properties for the production's lighting design. The system is also capable of extracting real-time RGB data from external video sources, precisely mapping virtual materials to the physical LED nodes on the sphere's surface.

Additionally, I managed the integration of multi-source motion assets, ensuring that lighting effects and mechanical displacements reached synchronized, high-precision simulation within the digital environment.

content.heading: Impact
content.body: This pre-visualization system allowed the team to verify lighting and spatial aesthetics in a virtual space prior to physical deployment, significantly enhancing the precision and efficiency of the final lighting execution.

exhibition.name: 2026 NTT Arts NOVA — 豪華朗機工《最後一問》THE LAST QUESTION
